principle.name: Validate with MCP before fixing
principle.description: Use MCP tools to directly control the game and reproduce issues before attempting any fix.
principle.rationale: MCP provides deterministic, repeatable validation that does not rely on LLM behavior.

example.good: Use MCP to set up game state, execute actions, and verify bug manifestation
example.bad: Assume the bug exists without direct validation

principle.name: Create tests that would have caught the bug
principle.description: Write tests that would have detected this bug before it was fixed.
principle.rationale: Prevents the same bug from recurring undetected in future development.

principle.name: Find root cause before fixing
principle.description: Never apply a fix until you understand WHY the bug happens. Symptom fixes mask real problems.
principle.rationale: Without root cause analysis, fixes are often wrong or create new bugs.

example.good: Read error → read source → check gotchas → form hypothesis → verify → fix
example.bad: Read error → immediately change code to suppress the error

principle.name: Minimal fix only
principle.description: Change the smallest amount of code needed to fix the bug. One line is better than ten.
principle.rationale: Large changes introduce new bugs and make regression testing harder.

principle.name: Run ALL tests before declaring fixed
principle.description: A fix is not complete until every test in the project passes.
principle.rationale: Bug fixes frequently cause regressions in unrelated areas.

principle.name: Add regression test if missing
principle.description: If caught this bug but did not, add a test that would have caught it.
principle.rationale: Prevents the same bug from recurring undetected.

pattern.name: game_state_setup
pattern.description: Use MCP to set up specific game scenarios for validation

steps.step: Use MCP tools to position entities at specific coordinates
Use MCP tools to set entity stats (health, power, etc.)
Use MCP tools to add items to inventory or spawn on map

pattern.name: action_execution
pattern.description: Execute game actions via MCP or direct method calls

steps.step: Use game.main_loop() for full turn processing
Verify state changes after action execution

pattern.name: deterministic_game_test
pattern.description: Create tests that do not rely on LLM behavior

example.code: def test_player_movement_blocked_by_wall(): from darkdelve import Game game = Game() game.initialize() # Position player near a wall game.player.x, game.player.y = 1, 1 # Assuming wall at (2,1) # Try to move right into wall initial_x = game.player.x game.process_action("d") # move right # Player should not have moved assert game.player.x == initial_x

def test_combat_resolver_damage(): from darkdelve import CombatResolver, Entity, Inventory player = Entity(x=0, y=0, char='@', color=(255,255,255), name="Player", hp=20, max_hp=20, power=10, defense=5, inventory=Inventory()) monster = Entity(x=1, y=0, char='g', color=(100,200,100), name="Goblin", hp=10, max_hp=10, power=3, defense=1, inventory=Inventory()) result = CombatResolver.resolve_attack(player, monster) assert result.damage > 0 assert monster.hp < 10

diagnosis_rules.rule: Read the complete error message and stack trace before reading code
Identify the exact file, line number, and function from the stack trace
Read at least 50 lines of context around the failure
Check architecture/gotchas.md for known pitfalls that match the symptom
Form a hypothesis before touching code: "The bug is caused by X because Y"
Verify the hypothesis by tracing the code path mentally
If you cannot determine root cause, ask for more information

pitfall.why_problemistic: Fixing the wrong cause leaves the bug unfixed
pitfall.correct_approach: Trace the code path carefully. Verify each step. Pick the most specific cause.

pitfall.why_problemistic: The fix changes behavior in an unrelated area
pitfall.correct_approach: After fix, run ALL tests. If any fail, determine if related to fix or pre-existing.

pitfall.description: Over-fixing (fixing things that are not broken)
pitfall.why_problemistic: Changes to working code introduce new bugs
pitfall.correct_approach: Only change what is directly causing the bug. Leave everything else alone.

pitfall.why_problemistic: LLM behavior is non-deterministic and may not reproduce bugs consistently
pitfall.correct_approach: Use MCP for deterministic bug validation and reproduction.

category.name: before_fix
category.item: Error message and stack trace read completely
Test created that would have caught the bug

category.name: after_fix
category.item: Regression test added if one was missing
Report produced with root cause, fix, and test results
